title: Bank deposit risk explained: What happens to your money if a lender fails to return it?
summary: Bank deposits are generally safe, but in case of bank failure, only ₹5 lakh per depositor is insured under the DICGC. This coverage applies to all accounts in a bank, with specific exclusions for certain deposits. Details here.&nbsp;

title: Tax-saving FDs vs regular FDs: Interest rates, lock-in and tax benefits compared
summary: Fixed deposits (FDs) are favored for stable returns, with banks in India offering rates up to 7%. Tax-saving FDs provide deductions under Section 80C but have a 5-year lock-in. Interest rates vary, with senior citizens receiving higher rates.

title: Sovereign Gold Bonds: RBI announces redemption price for 2018 series payable on 4 May; here's how much you gain
summary: The RBI has set the final redemption price for its Sovereign Gold Bond 2018-19 Series-I at ₹14,901 per unit, payable on 4 May 2026. Investors could see gains of nearly 386% from the issue price of ₹3,114 per unit.

title: Income tax: How much cash can you deposit to prepay home loan principal without attracting I-T notice?
summary: Income tax rules discourage cash transactions, limiting annual deposits to ₹10 lakh. Experts recommend using savings accounts for cash deposits

title: How to claim stuck NPS funds with interest? Step-by-step guide for subscribers
summary: Unclaimed National Pension System contributions can be retrieved through a mechanism specified by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority. Full details here.

title: Family floater vs separate policy: What’s best health insurance policy for your elderly parents?
summary: Family floater health insurance premiums are primarily calculated based on the age of the eldest member covered. Other key factors are - sum insured, medical history of family members, and family size&nbsp;

title: PPF maturity rules: What investors should do when their account matures?
summary: As PPF accounts reach maturity, holders must choose to withdraw the total amount, extend for five years, or continue without extra deposits. Here's what each option offers.

title: Estate planning: How is father’s property divided among sons and daughters? Can a sister claim her brother’s property?
summary: A married woman's share in ancestral property is influenced by laws, family dynamics, and social norms. Key laws - the Indian Succession Act and Hindu Succession Act emphasise equal rights for daughters and sons in inheritance matters.

title: Why has my ₹90 lakh home loan's principal reduced by only ₹6 lakh despite paying 60 EMIs? I asked ChatGPT
summary: I asked ChatGPT why the principal of my ₹90 lakh home loan hasn't decreased much despite paying off ₹45 lakh via 60 EMIs. Here is what AI said.

title: 25-year old techie shares how he built a ₹20 lakh portfolio in 3 years while earning under ₹10 lpa
summary: A 25-year-old tech professional built a ₹20 lakh investment portfolio in three years on a salary under ₹10 lakh. He attributed his success to disciplined saving, investing 80-85% of his income, and living with parents to minimize expenses.

title: DMart Q4 Results: Avenue Supermarts cons PAT jumps 19% to Rs 656 crore, revenue too rises 19%
summary: DMart Q4 Results: Avenue Supermarts, the operator of DMart, on Saturday reported a net profit of Rs 656.6 crore for the March quarter of FY26, marking a jump of 19% from Rs 550.90 crore posted in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year. title: CDSL Q4 Results: Cons PAT declines 20% to Rs 80 crore, Rs 12.75/share dividend declared
summary: CDSL Q4 Results: Central Depository Services Limited reported a mixed Q4 FY26 performance, with revenue growing 17.1% to Rs 262.85 crore, but net profit declining 20% to Rs 80.22 crore. The company surpassed 18 crore demat accounts, highlighting growing investor trust despite profitability pressures

title: FII outflows not driven by lack of AI and high taxes, says Shankar Sharma. Here’s why
summary: Foreign investors are not avoiding India due to AI exposure or tax issues, according to investor Shankar Sharma. Data shows markets with little AI have outperformed. India's own past performance contradicts the AI dependency narrative. FII flows and taxation show a weak link to market direction. Glo

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Results: PAT jumps 13% to Rs 4,026.55 crore, NII rises 8%
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Results: Kotak Mahindra Bank on Saturday reported a net profit of Rs 4,026.55 crore for the March quarter of FY26, marking a jump of 13.3% from Rs 3,552 crore posted in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year.

title: Nifty stays range bound as volatility rises; breakout awaited
summary: Nifty 50 traded within a narrow range, closing slightly higher amid rising volatility and weak momentum indicators. Markets remain in consolidation, with key resistance and support levels intact, while sector rotation suggests selective opportunities despite the absence of strong directional trends.

title: AI spending boom soars but no returns for big tech giants, warns Jefferies’ Chris Wood
summary: Rising AI investments by global tech giants are straining cash flows, with capex nearing record levels relative to operating cash flow. Despite aggressive spending, monetisation remains uncertain, prompting concerns over profitability. Analysts warn the AI sector may resemble capital-intensive indus

title: Largecap IT stocks as a value play? BNP Paribas’ Kumar Rakesh issues a reality check
summary: BNP Paribas analyst Kumar Rakesh cautions that weak FY27 guidance, AI-led disruption, and stalled client spending challenge the IT sector’s “value” narrative. While buybacks and dividends provide support, earnings risks remain elevated, making selective stock picking crucial for investors.

title: Microcap stocks steal show in April, outpace, Nifty, smallcap, midcaps, largecaps. What’s driving the surge?
summary: Indian equities surged in April 2026, led by strong gains in mid, small, and microcaps despite persistent macro risks, foreign outflows, and currency weakness. Market performance diverged from fundamentals, which remained stable, suggesting price-driven momentum rather than earnings-led improvement
